synopses.short: Phil Daniels and Rebecca Lucy Taylor aka Self Esteem are here!
synopses.medium: Scott's got your morning sorted... and Rebecca Lucy Taylor (Self Esteem) and Phil Daniels are here!
synopses.long:
Phil Daniels gets up when he wants... except on Wednesdays when he has to come and see Scott Mills. He's on the show with Rebecca Lucy Taylor aka Self Esteem. They're chatting about their new play 'Teeth 'n' Smiles'.  

Tina Daheley has the latest headlines, and Ellie Brennan will bring you up-to-date traffic and travel news. There’s sport, a Pause For Thought, the Birthday Game and your chance to get your hands on Scott’s Golden Toaster! 

Get involved by emailing scott.mills@bbc.co.uk – and listen live weekdays from 6.30am! 

To listen on most smart speakers, just say: "Ask BBC Sounds to play Scott Mills.” 

Produced by BBC Audio for BBC Radio 2.

synopses.short: Join Tom McKinney every Monday to Friday for Radio 3's Breakfast show.
synopses.medium: Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
synopses.long:
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3’s Breakfast show live from Salford. With birdsong, Bach Before 7 and the best in classical music. You can contact the show by emailing 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.

To listen on most smart speakers, just say, 'Ask BBC Sounds to play 3 Breakfast’.

synopses.short: Irene has been sorting through her late aunt’s possessions.
synopses.medium: Irene has been sorting through the possessions belonging to her late aunt. Starring Prunella Scales and Patricia Routledge.
synopses.long:
Irene has been sorting through her late Aunt Doris's possessions.

Grandmothers Irene Spencer and Vera Small continue their tempestuous correspondence. 

They started to look beyond at the future of the planet - but as ever, soon manage to bring their focus back to themselves!

Starring Prunella Scales and Patricia Routledge.

Written by Lou Wakefield and Carole Hayman

Vera Small .... Patricia Routledge
Irene Spencer .... Prunella Scales

Producer: Liz Webb

First broadcast on BBC Radio 4 in March 2008.

synopses.short: In 2006, a Spanish-language film became a cult classic and took the world by storm
synopses.medium: Ivana Baquero was 11 when she starred in Guillermo del Toro’s film which won awards around the world.
synopses.long:
In 2006, Ivana Baquero starred in Guillermo del Toro’s Spanish-language film Pan’s Labyrinth.  

The film tells the story of an 11-year-old girl who meets mythical creatures on a quest to achieve immortality and return to the underworld as a princess. 

However, it isn’t your typical fairy tale. Set in 1944, against the backdrop of Franco’s fascist Spain, the film is hard-hitting and at times violent. 

Ivana Baquero speaks to Tim O’Callaghan about starring in the film when she was an 11-year-old. 

Eye-witness accounts brought to life by archive. Witness History is for those fascinated by and curious about the past. We take you to the events that have shaped our world through the eyes of the people who were there. 

For nine minutes every day, we take you back in time and all over the world, to examine wars, coups, scientific discoveries, cultural moments and much more. 

Recent episodes explore everything from how the Excel spreadsheet was developed, the creation of cartoon rabbit Miffy and how the sound barrier was broken.

We look at the lives of some of the most famous leaders, artists, scientists and personalities in history, including: the moment Reagan and Gorbachev met in Geneva, Haitian singer Emerante de Pradines’ life and Omar Sharif’s legendary movie entrance in Lawrence of Arabia.

You can learn all about fascinating and surprising stories, like the invention of a stent which has saved lives around the world; the birth of the G7; and the meeting of Maldives’ ministers underwater. We cover everything from World War Two and Cold War stories to Black History Month and our journeys into space.

(Photo: Ivana Baquero at the premiere of Pan’s Labyrinth in 2006. Credit: Reuters/Fred Prouser)

synopses.short: Evil Pea pretends to be a famous pop star to take over the Tato Radio Show.
synopses.long: DJ Supertato is showing off his brand‑new sound‑effect board when he and Carrot get a surprise call from a powerful celebrity manager who tells them they can have an exclusive interview with the most famous veggie pop star, Starfruit! When Starfruit arrives at the radio studio, she sounds strangely familiar and has many demands that lead Supertato and Carrot out of the studio, where she immediately locks them out and reveals that she’s actually Evil Pea and is taking over the Tato Radio Show!
